title: MP donates pails and chairs
author: Mussa Kabichi
body: The Member of Parliament for Mangochi Monkey Bay Constituency, Hon. Gerald Kazembe has donated pails and chairs to Mponya Primary School in Monkey Bay Zone in a bid to improve sanitation and the overall learning environment for pupils.. The items were handed over to the school during a brief ceremony attended by learners and members of the surrounding community. School authorities said the donation will facilitate safe storage of drinking water, promote handwashing, and support efforts to maintain clean classrooms.. In a statement, Hon. Kazembe reaffirmed his commitment to advancing education development across the constituency, with plans to implement further interventions. Key among the priorities is the construction of teachers’ houses to enhance staff welfare and retention.. Headteacher Peter Bula welcomed the donation, describing it as both timely and impactful. He said the swift fulfillment of the pledge reflects strong commitment, adding that the support will contribute to improved learner health and a more conducive learning environment.. Members of the school community have also commended the initiative, noting that it represents a positive step toward ensuring a safer and healthier space for teaching and learning.

title: Gvt urged to raise bridge
body: The Member of Parliament for Mangochi North East, Overton Imedi, has urged the Ministry of Transport to rehabilitate and raise Mtumbwasi Bridge to prevent flooding. The bridge is currently unusable due to high water levels in the Shire River, making it difficult for people to cross for daily activities and access services. A teacher at Mangale Primary School in St Augustine Zone, Dorica Luhanga, said she finds it difficult to report for duties as she is forced to spend extra money to cross the river each day. Residents are now appealing for urgent government intervention, warning that continued delays will worsen hardships and disrupt livelihoods in the affected communities..
